headline: Kilcher Drops Bombshell Lawsuit Against Cameron and Disney Over 'Avatar' Character Design
lede: 'Yellowstone' star Q'orianka Kilcher alleges unauthorized use of her likeness in landmark intellectual property battle targeting Hollywood heavyweight James Cameron.
body:
In a provocative legal maneuver that could redefine actor image rights, Q'orianka Kilcher has filed a lawsuit against James Cameron and Disney, claiming the "Avatar" director extracted her facial features without consent for the Na'vi character Neytiri.

The lawsuit, filed in federal court, centers on Kilcher's performance in a 2005 independent film, which Cameron allegedly used as source material when designing the lead alien character portrayed by Zoe Saldana. Kilcher was 14 at the time of the original film's production.

Cameron's well-documented process of digital character modeling has long fascinated the entertainment industry. His groundbreaking techniques in "Avatar" revolutionized motion capture technology, creating hyper-realistic digital performances that blur traditional boundaries between actor and animated representation.

Legal experts suggest the case could establish critical precedents around digital likeness rights. The potential financial and creative implications for Hollywood's expanding digital production landscape remain substantial.

Representatives for Cameron and Disney have not yet publicly responded to the lawsuit's specific allegations.
key_facts: Claims unauthorized use of actress's facial features
